name: AdS holography vs cosmological-de Sitter universe
summary: The AdS/CFT correspondence (Maldacena 1997) is a non-perturbatively well-defined quantum-gravity-in-asymptotically-AdS framework; we live in an asymptotically de Sitter universe (Lambda > 0). No analogous boundary-CFT construction exists for de Sitter. The dS/CFT proposals (Strominger 2001) require a Euclidean-CFT-on-spatial-infinity dual, which has not been concretely realized in a top-down construction. Swampland-conjecture pressure (Obied-Ooguri-Spodyneiko-Vafa 2018, arXiv:1806.08362) suggests metastable de Sitter may not exist in consistent quantum gravity at all - if true, our observed Lambda > 0 universe needs a quintessence-like description, not a cosmological constant.

characteristic_numbers.note: Same value as in cosmological_constant_smallness, but here flagged as a *consistency* problem - the issue is not just the size but whether dS makes sense in quantum gravity at all.

principles_stressed.note: AdS/CFT presupposes a non-dynamical AdS boundary at infinity; an analogous structure for dS would be a spacelike boundary in the infinite future ('I^+'). The conceptual structure of QG-in-dS - which Hilbert space, which observables, which boundary conditions - remains debated. Causal patches (Banks-Fischler) suggest a finite-dimensional Hilbert space; static-patch holography would replace global by observer-dependent QG.

history: Maldacena (1997) AdS/CFT correspondence. Strominger (2001), 'The dS/CFT correspondence', JHEP 10, 034, hep-th/0106113. Banks-Fischler (2001), 'M-theory observables for cosmological space-times', hep-th/0102077. de Sitter conjecture: Obied, Ooguri, Spodyneiko, Vafa (2018), arXiv:1806.08362. Static-patch holography revival: Susskind 2021, Chandra-Penington-Witten 2023.

references.citation: Obied, Ooguri, Spodyneiko, Vafa, 'De Sitter Space and the Swampland', arXiv:1806.08362 (2018)
